home.title: Every Saudi reg. One captain on the answer.
home.subtitle: A fast, free reference library of Saudi civil-aviation regulations (GACAR), charts and study tools — with an AI flight instructor who cites the exact Part and section.
home.notAffiliated: Not affiliated with GACA. Always verify against the latest official GACA publication.

tools.title: Flight tools
tools.subtitle: A growing suite of fast, shareable calculators and references for everyday flying. Every result links to Captain Adel for an explanation grounded in GACAR.

crosswind.title: Crosswind & headwind
crosswind.intro: Resolve the reported wind into its crosswind and headwind components for a runway. The inputs ride the URL, so any setup is shareable.

densityAltitude.title: Density altitude
densityAltitude.intro: Pressure altitude, ISA deviation and density altitude from field elevation, the altimeter setting and the outside air temperature. A planning aid — performance figures come from the POH/AFM.

tas.title: True airspeed
tas.intro: Convert calibrated airspeed to true airspeed and Mach via the ISA standard atmosphere, from CAS, pressure altitude and OAT.

chat.title: Captain Adel
chat.status: AI flight instructor · cites the GACAR
chat.welcome: Captain Adel — your AI study assistant, trained on the GACAR. Ask about any Saudi regulation and he cites the exact Part and section. He can be wrong — always verify against the official source.
chat.placeholder: Ask about any GACAR Part or section…
chat.thinking: Captain Adel is checking the GACAR…
chat.notReady: I couldn't reach my engine just now, Captain. The assistant backend isn't connected in this build. Verify any regulation against the official GACA source at gaca.gov.sa.
chat.disclaimer: Captain Adel is an educational AI and can be wrong. Always verify against the official GACA source — not for operational use.

suggestions.s1: What are the VFR weather minima in controlled airspace?

library.title: The Saudi aviation reference, in one place.
library.subtitle: Every numbered GACAR Part, browsable and searchable. Our ingested copy — always verify against the official GACA publication.

document.readerPending: The in-app reader for this Part is being ported.
document.verifyAtGaca: Read the official GACAR at gaca.gov.sa

pressureAltitude.formula: Pressure altitude = elevation + (1013.25 − QNH) × 27.3 ft. The flight level is that pressure altitude in hundreds of feet.

isaTool.formula: ISA temperature = 15 °C − 1.98 °C per 1000 ft. The deviation is OAT minus that standard temperature.

altimeter.formula: QFE = QNH − elevation ÷ 27.3 hPa. Set QNH and the altimeter reads field elevation; set QFE and it reads zero on the ground.

cloudBase.formula: Convective cloud base ≈ (temperature − dew point) × 400 ft. A rough estimate for cumuliform cloud only.

mach.formula: Speed of sound a = 38.97 × √(OAT + 273.15) kt. Mach = TAS ÷ a.

climb.formula: Percent = ft/NM ÷ 60.76. Angle = atan(ft/NM ÷ 6076). Rate = ft/NM × groundspeed ÷ 60 ft/min.

turn.formula: A rate-one turn is 3°/s. Bank = atan(V × 0.0524 ÷ g); radius = V ÷ 0.0524. Rule of thumb: bank ≈ TAS/10 + 7.

windTable.empty: Enter wind and at least one runway.
windTable.formula: Each runway is resolved against the reported wind: crosswind = speed × sin(angle), headwind = speed × cos(angle).

hydroplaning.formula: Dynamic hydroplaning speed ≈ 9 × √(tyre pressure) kt for a rotating tyre. Onset also depends on water depth, tyre wear and surface texture.

runwayPerf.formula: Required = book × (1 + 10% per 1000 ft DA) × (1 + 5% per 1% upslope) × (1 + safety factor). These are conservative rules of thumb — the authoritative figures are the AFM/POH and your operator's data.

disclaimer.body: Fly GACA is an independent educational tool. It is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to the General Authority of Civil Aviation (GACA). The authoritative source for any regulation is always GACA. Always verify against the latest official GACA publication at gaca.gov.sa before relying on any information.

legal.updated: An educational reference — not a legal document.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.intro: Please read this before relying on anything you find here.

sections.p: Fly GACA is privately built for study and reference. It carries no official status and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to GACA.
Where Fly GACA shows a regulation it is pointing you to GACA's text. The official GACA publication always prevails over anything shown here.

sections.h: For study and reference only — not for operational use
sections.p: Nothing here is for flight planning, dispatch, navigation or maintenance sign-off. Use official, current sources for operations.
Aerodrome and chart data follows the 28-day AIRAC cycle and may be out of date; it is illustrative and not for operational use.

sections.h: No warranty of accuracy or completeness
sections.p: The content is machine-extracted from GACA PDFs and provided “as is”, without warranty of accuracy, completeness or fitness for any purpose.

sections.h: Captain Adel — the AI study assistant
sections.p: Captain Adel is an educational AI that cites sources but can be wrong. Treat its answers as a study aid and verify against the official GACAR.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.intro: By using Fly GACA you agree to these terms. Contact us at i@flygaca.com.

sections.p: An independent educational platform and reference library for Saudi civil-aviation regulation. It is not affiliated with GACA.
You may not rely on Fly GACA for flight planning, operations, dispatch or any operational decision. Use official current sources.
Do not use the service unlawfully, disrupt or scrape it, or misrepresent it as official or as carrying any regulatory status.
Fly GACA owns its name, brand and design. The underlying regulatory documents belong to GACA and are shown for reference.
We link to external sites (including GACA) for verification but are not responsible for their content or availability.
The service is provided “as is” and “as available”. To the extent permitted by law, Fly GACA is not liable for any loss arising from its use or reliance on it.
We may change the service and these terms at any time; continued use means acceptance. These terms are governed by the laws of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.

privacy.title: Privacy Notice
privacy.intro: How Fly GACA handles your data. Contact us at privacy@flygaca.com.

sections.p: Fly GACA operates flygaca.com as an independent educational reference. Reach us at privacy@flygaca.com.
A waitlist email if you provide one, your chat questions to Captain Adel, and basic technical logs for security and reliability.
To run the service, answer your questions, and keep it secure — on the basis of your consent and our legitimate interest.
User questions are personal data and are processed in-Kingdom: Google Cloud in Dammam (me-central2), in line with the PDPL.
We do not sell your data. We use processors (Firebase, Cloudflare) to operate the service, and disclose data only if required by law.
You may ask to be informed about, access, correct or delete your data, and withdraw consent. Contact privacy@flygaca.com.
We use minimal local storage (such as your language choice) and privacy-friendly analytics. We keep data only as long as needed for the purposes above.

about.title: About Fly GACA
about.lead: Every Saudi reg. One captain on the answer. Finding the right GACAR section should not be the hard part of flying in the Kingdom.

sections.p: A reference library of every numbered GACAR Part, topical handbooks, aerodromes and VFR charts — plus Captain Adel, an AI flight instructor that cites the exact section, and a set of flight tools and study material.
It is not GACA and not official. It does not replace the GACAR, the AIP, the AFM or operator manuals, and nothing here is for operational use.
A bilingual, RTL-aware Progressive Web App that works offline. Content is extracted from official GACA PDFs into a clean, searchable form that both full-text search and Captain Adel draw from.
Student pilots, instructors, cadets and licensed crew who actually use the regulations — in English and Arabic.
Wherever Fly GACA shows a regulation, it points to GACA. Always verify against the current official publication at gaca.gov.sa.

footer.tagline: Every Saudi reg. One captain on the answer. An independent educational reference for civil aviation in the Kingdom.
